page: Amazon Web Services mac2-m2pro.metal is a bare metal macOS Gen2 server powered by a dedicated Apple processor. The hardware configuration features 12 physical cores and 12 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz on the arm64_mac architecture. This design provides a single thread per core, ensuring dedicated execution resources without hyperthreading.

The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.67 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or a discrete GPU, meaning storage must be provisioned externally. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal instance provides dedicated Apple silicon hardware resources, making it a specialized resource density choice for macOS-specific environments. Tradeoffs include the lack of local storage and discrete GPU accelerators. This configuration is designed for workloads such as macOS and iOS application building, CI/CD pipelines, and testing environments requiring native Apple hardware.
description: Amazon Web Services mac2-m2pro.metal is a bare metal macOS Gen2 server featuring a dedicated Apple arm64_mac processor. The system delivers 12 physical cores and 12 vCPUs running at 3.5 GHz with a single thread per core, ensuring dedicated compute performance. It is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.67 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or a discrete GPU, requiring external storage solutions. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps, with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, this bare metal server offers specialized resource density for macOS environments, presenting a tradeoff in the lack of integrated local storage. It is engineered for native Apple development workloads, including iOS and macOS application compilation, CI/CD build pipelines, and automated testing.
